intent: In DATA_DIR there is a 10x matrix triplet for one PBMC sample — files prefixed GSM5746268_MGI0369_1_SLAB-C1-C1. Run a standard Seurat workflow in R: load, QC, normalize, PCA, neighbors, clustering, UMAP, and FindAllMarkers; show me a UMAP and the top markers per cluster.

blocks.text:
Good — I can see the 10x data: there are actually three samples (GSM5746268, GSM5746269, GSM5746270), each with the standard 10x MTX triplet (matrix, features, barcodes). The user asked specifically for the GSM5746268 sample, so I'll focus on that one.

Now let me load the Seurat recipe to confirm the workflow:

blocks.content: {"status": "ok", "name": "seurat-scrna", "description": "Authoritative single-cell RNA-seq QC + clustering + cluster annotation with R/Seurat (v5).", "body": "scRNA-seq QC + clustering + annotation with R/Seurat v5 (standard PBMC3k workflow). Provision: ensure_capability('Seurat'), then library(Seurat). THE THREE CHOICES THAT DEFINE THE RESULT \u2014 surface them with present_plan before committing: (1) QC thresholds (subset cutoffs on nFeature_RNA and percent.mt); (2) number of PCs/dims (read off the ElbowPlot); (3) clustering resolution (FindClusters). Steps: ReadMtx -> CreateSeuratObject(min.cells=3,min.features=200) -> PercentageFeatureSet('^MT-') -> subset QC -> NormalizeData -> FindVariableFeatures -> ScaleData -> RunPCA -> FindNeighbors -> FindClusters -> RunUMAP -> JoinLayers -> FindAllMarkers. Load ggplot2 and dplyr explicitly; never library(tidyverse)."}

blocks.text: Perfect. The recipe is clear. Let me now present a plan before executing the workflow:

input.title: Seurat scRNA-seq workflow (GSM5746268)
input.rationale: Load the 10x MTX triplet, run standard Seurat QC → clustering → UMAP → marker identification, and visualize results.

steps.title: Load 10x MTX triplet into R/Seurat
steps.description: Use ReadMtx() to load matrix, features, barcodes files and create a Seurat object with default filtering (min.cells=3, min.features=200).

steps.title: QC: compute mitochondrial % and inspect
steps.description: Add percent.mt annotation via PercentageFeatureSet on MT- genes; show a violin/scatter plot of nFeature_RNA and percent.mt to determine subset thresholds.

steps.title: Subset, normalize, find HVGs
steps.description: Subset on QC thresholds (apply conservative defaults: nFeature_RNA 200–5000, percent.mt < 20%), NormalizeData (log), FindVariableFeatures (2000).

steps.title: Scale, PCA, neighbors
steps.description: ScaleData, RunPCA (default 30), inspect ElbowPlot, then FindNeighbors and FindClusters at resolution 0.8.

steps.title: UMAP and FindAllMarkers
steps.description: RunUMAP on PCA reduction, then FindAllMarkers with Wilcoxon test to identify top markers per cluster.

steps.title: Visualize: UMAP and top markers table
steps.description: Save UMAP plot colored by cluster identity. Display top 5 markers per cluster in a summary table.

assumptions: Gene names are in column 2 of features file (standard 10x format).
QC thresholds: nFeature_RNA 200–5000, percent.mt < 20% (conservative defaults; user can adjust if needed).
PCA dimensions: inspect ElbowPlot visually; use ~20 if no clear elbow.
